pi_items.source: install_openice.sh line 66 (git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp.git) and export_result.sh line 18 (find $PROJECT_ROOT -path "*/build/test-results/test/TEST-*.xml")
Standard Gradle test output and verifier.py line 68 checks report_content > 100 (snippet check, full file would be larger)
Gradle documentation confirms standard JUnit XML output pattern TEST-*.xml in build/test-results/test/ directory. Verified via web search on Gradle JUnit test output conventions.
export_result.sh line 98 uses this exact command to extract git commit hash. Verified via Git documentation that --oneline outputs abbreviated 7-character commit hash.
install_openice.sh line 74 (chmod +x /opt/openice/mdpnp/gradlew) and setup_openice.sh line 36 (./gradlew). OpenICE 2.0 release notes confirm Gradle 7.6 integration.
install_openice.sh lines 13-18 install openjdk-17-jdk and openjdk-17-jre. setup_openice.sh lines 10 and 36 set JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64. OpenICE 2.0 requires Java 17.
install_openice.sh line 66 (git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp.git) and verified via web search of OpenICE official GitHub repository.

pi_items.verified_value: Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment (ICE) - a medical device interoperability platform implementing ANSI/AAMI 2700-1 standard
pi_items.source: Web search results from official OpenICE documentation and mdpnp GitHub organization page
export_result.sh line 18 uses -path "*/build/test-results/test/TEST-*.xml". Gradle documentation confirms standard output at build/test-results/<taskName>/ with TEST-*.xml filenames.

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is a real open-source medical device interoperability platform from Harvard Medical School's MD PnP Program, hosted at https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp. Version 2.0 uses Gradle 7.6 for building and testing with Java 17. The project root will be installed at /opt/openice/mdpnp, and Gradle will automatically generate JUnit XML test results matching the pattern TEST-*.xml in build/test-results/test/ directories. Git commit information can be obtained via 'git log --oneline -1' command, which outputs a 7-character abbreviated hash. The test execution report should be written to /home/ga/Desktop/test_execution_report.txt with minimum 200 bytes of content. All metadata path and command specifications are accurate.
